\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage{booktabs}

\title{A Sample Paper with Common LaTeX Errors}
\author{Test Author}

\begin{document}

\maketitle

\section{Introduction}

This is a correct section with no errors. The parameter $\alpha$ controls the learning rate,
and $x_i$ denotes the $i$-th input sample. We use $\mathbf{W}$ to represent weight matrices.

\begin{equation}
  \mathcal{L} = -\sum_{i=1}^{N} y_i \log(\hat{y}_i) + (1 - y_i) \log(1 - \hat{y}_i)
\end{equation}

\section{Typo Errors}

% ERROR: typo in \begin
\beginn{itemize}
  \item First item with a typo in the command: \hlin should be \hline
  \item Another typo: \usepacakge is wrong
  \item And: \textbfseries should be \textbf
\endd{itemize}

\section{Math Mode Errors}

% ERROR: math symbols used outside math mode
The parameters \alpha and \beta control the shape of the distribution.
We can write x_i to denote the i-th observation, and x^2 for squared values.

% ERROR: double superscript
\begin{equation}
  x^a^b = \sum_{i=1}^{n} y_i^2
\end{equation}

\section{Bracket Errors}

% ERROR: missing closing brace
\textbf{This text has missing closing brace.

% ERROR: extra closing brace
This text has an extra closing brace.}

\section{Environment Errors}

% ERROR: begin/end mismatch
\begin{figure}
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{example.png}
  \caption{An example figure.}
\end{table}

% ERROR: wrong nesting order
\begin{table}
  \begin{tabular}{ll}
    A & B \\
    C & D \\
  \end{table}
  \end{tabular}

\section{Table Errors}

% ERROR: too many alignment tabs
\begin{tabular}{ll}
  A & B & C \\
  D & E \\
\end{tabular}

% ERROR: misplaced \hline outside tabular
Some text before the table.

\hline

\section{Spacing Errors}

% ERROR: misplaced \\ outside tabular/equation
This is a paragraph with a bad line break.\\
This continues after the break.

\section{Label Errors}

% ERROR: multiply defined label
\label{sec:intro}

\section{Duplicate Label}
\label{sec:intro}

\section{Reference Errors}

% ERROR: undefined reference and citation
As shown in Section \ref{sec:missing}, our method outperforms \cite{undefined2024}.

\begin{figure}
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.5\textwidth]{plot.pdf}
  \caption{Results on benchmark datasets.}
  \label{fig:results}
\end{figure}

% This reference is valid
Figure \ref{fig:results} shows our main results.

\end{document}
